items.headline: City approves Seeds of Peace Monument artwork at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: San José City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by artist Mario Chiodo to be located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The monument memorializes survivors and reflects the city's commitment to public art in one of its major waterfront spaces.

items.headline: Council sets 2026-27 budget for Tully Road Eastridge business district
items.summary: The City Council took action on the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District's 2026-27 budget and annual assessment, which funds improvements and services in that commercial corridor.

items.headline: Cupertino Updates Health and Safety Element of General Plan
items.summary: City Council is updating the Health and Safety Element of the Cupertino General Plan (2015-2040 Community Vision), which guides how the city addresses public health, emergency preparedness, and safety infrastructure over the next 15 years. This update will reflect current community needs and may influence zoning, development standards, and city services.

items.headline: City Council appoints new permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. This fills a leadership position responsible for day-to-day city operations and implementing council policies.

items.headline: New duplex permitted on Old Piedmont Road
items.summary: A new two-story, two-unit duplex has been permitted at 2174 Old Piedmont Rd, representing a modest increase in housing supply. At just over $1 million, this is the largest residential project in this batch and one of few multi-unit new construction permits.

items.headline: City discusses draft Urban Water Master Plan and drought contingencies
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will review Sunnyvale's Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. This shapes how the city manages water supply and responds to future droughts.

items.headline: Spring grants open for community events and neighborhood projects
items.summary: The city is considering 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program applications in the spring cycle on May 18. Residents and local groups can learn which projects have been selected for funding.

items.headline: City develops strategy for residential electrification
items.summary: Palo Alto is setting proposed principles for electrification programs to help residential properties comply with air district regulations, signaling a shift toward electric appliances and away from natural gas in homes.
